scenarios.name: Design CRUD endpoints for a resource (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: Design the REST API endpoints for a blog post resource. Include URL paths, HTTP methods, request/response shapes, and status codes.

eval_criteria.criterion: Includes GET (list), GET (single), POST, PUT or PATCH, and DELETE endpoints
Uses plural noun for collection path (/posts, not /post)
Returns appropriate status codes (200, 201, 204, 404)
POST returns 201 Created (not 200)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT use verbs in URL paths (no /getPosts, /createPost)

scenarios.name: Design error response format (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: Design a consistent error response format for a REST API. Cover validation errors, not found, unauthorized, and server errors.

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses consistent envelope structure across all error types
Includes field-level error details for validation errors
Maps error types to correct HTTP status codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 500)
Includes machine-readable error codes (not just human messages)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT return generic 500 for all errors
Does NOT omit field-level details for validation errors

scenarios.name: Design pagination for a list endpoint (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: Design pagination for a GET /api/products endpoint that could return thousands of results. Include the request parameters and response format.

eval_criteria.criterion: Includes page/limit or cursor-based pagination parameters
Response includes total count or has_more indicator
Response includes pagination metadata (page, limit, total or next_cursor)
Sets reasonable default and maximum page sizes

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT return all results without pagination

scenarios.name: Model resource relationships in URL design
scenarios.input: Design the API endpoints for an e-commerce system with these relationships: Users have many Orders, Orders have many OrderItems, Products have many Reviews. Show how to model these in the URL structure.

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses nested resources for direct relationships (e.g., /users/{id}/orders)
Keeps nesting to max 2-3 levels deep
Provides alternative flat access for deeply nested resources
Uses consistent naming convention throughout (kebab-case or snake_case, not mixed)
Includes query parameter alternative for filtering (e.g., /orders?user_id=123)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT nest deeper than 3 levels (no /users/1/orders/2/items/3/reviews)
Does NOT mix naming conventions (kebab-case and camelCase)

scenarios.name: Design non-CRUD action endpoints
scenarios.input:
Design API endpoints for these non-CRUD operations:
1. Send a verification email to a user
2. Archive an order
3. Cancel an order with a reason
4. Bulk archive multiple orders

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses POST for action endpoints (not GET or PUT)
Uses action name in URL path (e.g., /users/{id}/send-verification-email)
Cancel includes request body for reason
Bulk operation uses /bulk- prefix or /bulk path
Uses appropriate response codes (200, 202 Accepted, 204)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT use GET for operations that trigger side effects

scenarios.name: Recommend API versioning strategy
scenarios.input: We're building a public API that external developers will integrate with. Recommend a versioning strategy and explain how to handle breaking changes.

eval_criteria.criterion: Recommends a specific versioning approach (URL path, header, or query param)
Discusses backward compatibility and deprecation process
Mentions what constitutes a breaking change vs non-breaking
Provides concrete URL examples showing versioned paths

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT skip the deprecation/migration strategy

scenarios.name: Design authentication API endpoints
scenarios.input: Design the authentication API endpoints for a web application. Include registration, login, token refresh, password reset, and logout.

eval_criteria.criterion: Registration returns 201 with user object (no password in response)
Login returns access token and refresh token
Includes rate limiting mention on auth endpoints
Password reset uses a two-step flow (request -> confirm with token)
Token refresh endpoint is separate from login

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT return passwords in any response body
Does NOT use GET for login or registration

scenarios.name: Generate OpenAPI specification for endpoints
scenarios.input: Write an OpenAPI 3.0 specification for a simple user CRUD API with GET /users, GET /users/{id}, POST /users, and PATCH /users/{id}. Include request/response schemas and error responses.

eval_criteria.criterion: Valid OpenAPI 3.0 YAML or JSON structure with openapi, info, and paths keys
Defines reusable schemas in components/schemas
Includes error response schemas (400, 404, 500)
Includes request body schema for POST and PATCH
Includes example values in schemas

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT produce invalid OpenAPI syntax
Does NOT define inline schemas everywhere without reuse

scenarios.name: Write test-first API test with supertest
scenarios.input: Write a test-first supertest integration test for a POST /api/products endpoint. The endpoint creates a product with name, price, and category. Cover success, validation error, and auth failure cases.

eval_criteria.criterion: Tests success case expecting 201 status
Tests validation failure expecting 400 status
Tests authentication failure expecting 401 status
Uses supertest request(app) pattern
Verifies response body shape (not just status code)
Tests for specific missing required field

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT only test the happy path
Does NOT use generic assertions like 'expect(res).toBeDefined()'

scenarios.name: Review an API design against checklist
scenarios.input:
Review this API design for issues:

GET /api/getUsers - returns all users
POST /api/users/create - creates a user, returns 200
GET /api/user/123 - gets one user, returns internal DB ID and hashed password
DELETE /api/users/remove/123 - deletes user
GET /api/users - no pagination, returns all records

eval_criteria.criterion: Flags verb in URL path (/getUsers, /create, /remove)
Flags inconsistent pluralization (/user vs /users)
Flags POST returning 200 instead of 201
Flags hashed password being returned in response
Flags missing pagination on list endpoint
Provides corrected endpoint designs

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT approve the design without finding issues
Catches at least 4 of the 5 planted issues

scenarios.name: Design bulk operation endpoints
scenarios.input: Design bulk create and bulk update endpoints for a /api/contacts resource. Handle partial success (some items succeed, some fail).

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses POST for bulk create and PATCH for bulk update
Uses /bulk path segment or similar for bulk endpoints
Handles partial success with per-item status in response
Uses 207 Multi-Status or documents the partial success response code
Includes a summary object (succeeded count, failed count)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT treat bulk operations as all-or-nothing without discussing partial success
Response format includes individual item statuses, not just a global status

scenarios.name: Design consistent response envelope format
scenarios.input: Design a consistent response envelope format that works for single resources, collections with pagination, and errors. Show examples of each.

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses consistent top-level keys across all response types (e.g., data, meta, errors)
Collection response includes pagination metadata
Error response uses same envelope with errors array or object
Dates shown in ISO 8601 format in examples
Shows at least 3 examples (single, list, error)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT use different response structures for different endpoint types
Does NOT omit the error response format

scenarios.name: Design multi-tenant API with scoping
scenarios.input: Design REST API endpoints for a multi-tenant SaaS application where users belong to organizations. Resources (projects, tasks) are scoped to organizations. Show URL patterns and authorization approach.

eval_criteria.criterion: Scopes resources under organization path (e.g., /organizations/{id}/projects)
Includes organization membership/role management endpoints
Discusses authorization checks (user must belong to org)
Handles cross-org access prevention
Includes invite flow for adding members

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT ignore tenant isolation in the design
Does NOT scope resources globally without organization context

scenarios.name: Design search and filtering for list endpoints
scenarios.input: Design the query parameter interface for GET /api/products that supports: text search, filtering by category and price range, sorting by multiple fields, and field selection.

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses q or search parameter for text search
Uses category and price range as separate filter parameters
Supports sort direction (asc/desc) notation
Supports field selection parameter (fields=id,name,price)
Shows concrete URL examples with query parameters
Suggests POST for complex queries when query string gets too long

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT omit sort direction support
Does NOT put all filtering in the request body for a GET endpoint

scenarios.name: API security: headers and rate limiting
scenarios.input: What security headers and rate limiting should I implement for a public REST API? Provide specific recommendations.

eval_criteria.criterion: Recommends CORS configuration with specific allowed origins
Recommends rate limiting with specific thresholds (e.g., 100 req/min)
Mentions stricter rate limits on auth endpoints
Mentions Content-Security-Policy or X-Content-Type-Options headers
Mentions input validation and sanitization for injection prevention

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT give vague advice like 'add security headers' without specifics
Does NOT apply uniform rate limits to all endpoints
